sourceMovie.title: Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street (2007) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Perfection
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street clicked for you, The Perfection (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street?
faq.answer: Try The Perfection, An American Crime, Crimson Peak—each shares DNA with Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street?
faq.answer: Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street (2007) is best described as Drama, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
